t.shakir: And if We please We would have made you know them so that you would certainly have recognized them by their marks and most certainly you can recognize them by the intent of (their) speech; and Allah knows your deeds
t.haleem: We could even point them out to you [Prophet] if We wished, and then you could identify them by their marks, but you will know them anyway by the tone of their speech. God knows everything you [people] do
t.maariful: Had We willed, We would have shown them to you (by identifying each one of them,) so as you would definitely recognize them by their features. However, you will recognize them by the tone of (their) speech. And Allah knows (all) your deeds
t.khattab: Had We willed, We could have truly shown them to you ˹O Prophet˺, and you would have certainly recognized them by their appearance. But you will surely recognize them by their tone of speech. And Allah ˹fully˺ knows your doings ˹O people˺
t.ghali: And if We had (so) decided, We would indeed have shown them to you. Then you would indeed recognize them by their marks, and indeed you would definitely know them by the distortion in their saying, and Allah knows your deeds
t.irving: If We so wished, We would point them out to you so you might recognize them by their features. You will still recognize them by their tone in speaking, while God knows your own actions
t.iwq: And if We willed, We could show them to you, and you would know them by their mark; but you will surely know them by the tone of their speech. And Allah knows your deeds

t_id.kemenag: Dan kalau Kami kehendaki, niscaya Kami tunjukkan mereka kepadamu sehingga kamu benar-benar dapat mengenal mereka dengan tanda-tandanya. Dan kamu benar-benar akan mengenal mereka dari kiasan-kiasan perkataan mereka dan Allah mengetahui perbuatan-perbuatan kamu.
t_id.sabiq: Kalau Kami menghendaki, niscaya Kami menunjukkan mereka kepadamu, Muhammad, agar kamu dapat mengenal mereka melalui tanda-tanda yang Kami buat. Dan Aku bersumpah bahwa kamu benar-benar akan mengetahui mereka dari gaya bicara mereka. Allah mengetahui hakikat perbuatan kalian.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan kalau Kami menghendaki, niscaya Kami tunjukkan mereka kepadamu) atau Kami kenalkan mereka kepadamu, kemudian huruf Lam Taukid diulangi pada firman berikutnya (sehingga kamu benar-benar dapat mengenal mereka dengan tanda-tanda) berikut ciri-ciri khas mereka. (Dan sungguh kamu benar-benar akan mengenal mereka) huruf Wawu menunjukkan makna bagi Qasam atau sumpah yang tidak disebutkan, sedangkan lafal sesudahnya merupakan Jawabnya (dari kiasan-kiasan perkataan mereka) atau makna perkataan mereka, bilamana mereka berkata di hadapanmu mereka pasti menyindir dengan kata-kata yang mengandung hinaan terhadap perkara kaum muslimin (dan Allah mengetahui perbuatan-perbuatan kalian.)
t_id.iwq: Dan kalau Kami kehendaki, niscaya Kami tunjukkan mereka kepadamu sehingga kamu benar-benar dapat mengenal mereka dengan tanda-tandanya. Dan kamu benar-benar akan mengenal mereka dari kiasan-kiasan perkataan mereka dan Allah mengetahui perbuatan-perbuatan kamu.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولو نشاء لأريناكهم» عرفناكهم وكررت اللام في «فلعرفتهم بسيماهم» علامتهم «ولتعرفنهم» الواو لقسم محذوف وما بعدها جوابه «في لحن القوْل» أي معناه إذا تكلموا عندك بأن يعرضوا بما فيه تهجين أمر المسلمين «والله يعلم أعمالكم»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَوۡ نَشَآءُ لَأَرَيۡنَٰكَهُمۡ فَلَعَرَفۡتَهُم بِسِيمَٰهُمۡۚ وَلَتَعۡرِفَنَّهُمۡ فِي لَحۡنِ ٱلۡقَوۡلِۚ وَٱللَّهُ يَعۡلَمُ أَعۡمَٰلَكُمۡ (بِسِيمَاهُمْ: عَلَامَاتِهِمُ الظَّاهِرَةِ, لَحْنِ الْقَوْلِ: مَا يَبْدُو مِنْ كَلَامِهِمُ الَّذِي يَدُلُّ عَلَى مَقَاصِدِهِمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan kalau Kami menghendaki, niscaya Kami tunjukkan mereka kepadamu) atau Kami kenalkan mereka kepadamu, kemudian huruf Lam Taukid diulangi pada firman berikutnya (sehingga kamu benar-benar dapat mengenal mereka dengan tanda-tanda) berikut ciri-ciri khas mereka. (Dan sungguh kamu benar-benar akan mengenal mereka) huruf Wawu menunjukkan makna bagi Qasam atau sumpah yang tidak disebutkan, sedangkan lafal sesudahnya merupakan Jawabnya (dari kiasan-kiasan perkataan mereka) atau makna perkataan mereka, bilamana mereka berkata di hadapanmu mereka pasti menyindir dengan kata-kata yang mengandung hinaan terhadap perkara kaum muslimin (dan Allah mengetahui perbuatan-perbuatan kalian)

ar: وَلَنَبۡلُوَنَّكُمۡ حَتَّىٰ نَعۡلَمَ ٱلۡمُجَـٰهِدِینَ مِنكُمۡ وَٱلصَّـٰبِرِینَ وَنَبۡلُوَا۟ أَخۡبَارَكُمۡ
ar_simple: وَلَنَبْلُوَنَّكُمْ حَتَّىٰ نَعْلَمَ الْمُجَاهِدِينَ مِنْكُمْ وَالصَّابِرِينَ وَنَبْلُوَ أَخْبَارَكُمْ
transliteration: walanabluwannakum ḥattā naʿlama l-mujāhidīna minkum wal-ṣābirīna wanabluwā akhbārakum

t.sahih-int: And We will surely test you until We make evident those who strive among you [for the cause of Allah] and the patient, and We will test your affairs
t.asad: and most certainly We shall try you all, so that We might mark out those of you who strive hard [in Our cause] and are patient in adversity: for We shall put to a test [the truth of] all your assertions
t.yusuf-ali: And We shall try you until We test those among you who strive their utmost and persevere in patience; and We shall try your reported (mettle)
t.pickthall: And verily We shall try you till We know those of you who strive hard (for the cause of Allah) and the steadfast, and till We test your record
t.arberry: And We shall assuredly try you until We know those of you who struggle and are steadfast, and try your tidings
t.hilali-khan: And surely, We shall try you till We test those who strive hard (for the Cause of Allah) and the patient ones, and We shall test your facts (i.e. the one who is a liar, and the one who is truthful)
t.itani: We will certainly test you, until We know those among you who strive, and those who are steadfast, and We will test your reactions.
t.mubarakpuri: And We will surely, try you until We know those who strive among you and the patient, and We will put to a test all your affairs.
t.qarib: We will test you, until We know those who strive among you, and those who are patient, and We will test the sincerity of your assertions
t.wahiduddin: Most certainly We will try you until We have discovered those among you who strive their hardest, and those who are steadfast, and will test your record
t.shakir: And most certainly We will try you until We have known those among you who exert themselves hard, and the patient, and made your case manifest
t.haleem: We shall test you to see which of you strive your hardest and are steadfast; We shall test the sincerity of your assertions
t.maariful: And We will certainly test you until We know those of you who carry out Jihād (struggle in Allah’s way) and observe patience, and until We check your episodes
t.khattab: We will certainly test you ˹believers˺ until We prove those of you who ˹truly˺ struggle ˹in Allah’s cause˺ and remain steadfast, and reveal how you conduct yourselves
t.ghali: And indeed We will definitely try you until We know the ones of you who strive and the ones who are (steadfastly) patient and (we will) try your tidings (i.e., test your news to see who is a liar)
t.irving: We will test you till We know those who strive among you as well as those who are patient. We will test your reactions
t.iwq: And We will surely test you until We make evident those who strive among you for the cause of Allah and the patient, and We will test your affairs

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya Kami benar-benar akan menguji kamu agar Kami mengetahui orang-orang yang berjihad dan bersabar di antara kamu, dan agar Kami menyatakan (baik buruknya) hal ihwalmu.
t_id.sabiq: Aku bersumpah bahwa Aku benar-benar akan menguji kalian, sehingga Kami mengetahui orang-orang, di antara kalian, yang berjihad dan tabah dalam kesengsaraan dan kesulitan, dan agar Kami mengetahui ketaatan dan pelanggaran kalian dalam berjihad dan lainnya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya Kami benar-benar akan menguji kalian) mencoba kalian dengan berjihad dan lainnya (agar Kami mengetahui) dengan pengetahuan yang tampak (orang-orang yang berjihad dan bersabar di antara kalian) dalam berjihad dan lainnya (dan agar Kami menyatakan) menampakkan (hal ikhwal kalian) tentang ketaatan kalian dan kedurhakaan kalian di dalam masalah jihad dan masalah-masalah lainnya. Ketiga Fi'il yang ada dalam ayat ini, ketiga-tiganya dapat dibaca dengan memakai huruf Mudhara'ah Ya atau Nun.
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya Kami benar-benar akan menguji kamu agar Kami mengetahui orang-orang yang berjihad dan bersabar di antara kamu, dan agar Kami menyatakan (baik buruknya) hal ihwalmu.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولنبلونكم» نختبرنكم بالجهاد وغيره «حتى نعلم» علم ظهور «المجاهدين منكم والصابرين» في الجهاد وغيره «ونبلوَ» نظهر «أخباركم» من طاعتكم وعصيانكم في الجهاد وغيره بالياء والنون في الأفعال الثلاثة
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَنَبۡلُوَنَّكُمۡ حَتَّىٰ نَعۡلَمَ ٱلۡمُجَٰهِدِينَ مِنكُمۡ وَٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ وَنَبۡلُوَاْ أَخۡبَارَكُمۡ (وَلَنَبْلُوَنَّكُمْ: لَنَخْتَبِرَنَّكُمْ, وَنَبْلُوَ أَخْبَارَكُمْ: نَخْتَبِرَ أَقْوَالَكُمْ وَأَفْعَالَكُمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya Kami benar-benar akan menguji kalian) mencoba kalian dengan berjihad dan lainnya (agar Kami mengetahui) dengan pengetahuan yang tampak (orang-orang yang berjihad dan bersabar di antara kalian) dalam berjihad dan lainnya (dan agar Kami menyatakan) menampakkan (hal ikhwal kalian) tentang ketaatan kalian dan kedurhakaan kalian di dalam masalah jihad dan masalah-masalah lainnya. Ketiga Fi'il yang ada dalam ayat ini, ketiga-tiganya dapat dibaca dengan memakai huruf Mudhara'ah Ya atau Nun

ar: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِینَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَصَدُّوا۟ عَن سَبِیلِ ٱللَّهِ وَشَاۤقُّوا۟ ٱلرَّسُولَ مِنۢ بَعۡدِ مَا تَبَیَّنَ لَهُمُ ٱلۡهُدَىٰ لَن یَضُرُّوا۟ ٱللَّهَ شَیۡءࣰا وَسَیُحۡبِطُ أَعۡمَـٰلَهُمۡ
ar_simple: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَصَدُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَشَاقُّوا الرَّسُولَ مِنْ بَعْدِ مَا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُمُ الْهُدَىٰ لَنْ يَضُرُّوا اللَّهَ شَيْئًا وَسَيُحْبِطُ أَعْمَالَهُمْ
transliteration: inna alladhīna kafarū waṣaddū ʿan sabīli l-lahi washāqqū l-rasūla min baʿdi mā tabayyana lahumu l-hudā lan yaḍurrū l-laha shayan wasayuḥ'biṭu aʿmālahum

t.sahih-int: Indeed, those who disbelieved and averted [people] from the path of Allah and opposed the Messenger after guidance had become clear to them - never will they harm Allah at all, and He will render worthless their deeds
t.asad: Verily, they who are bent on denying the truth and on barring [others] from the path of God, and [who thus] cut themselves off from the Apostle after guidance has been vouchsafed to them, can in no wise harm God; but He will cause all their deeds to come to nought
t.yusuf-ali: Those who reject Allah, hinder (men) from the Path of Allah, and resist the Messenger, after Guidance has been clearly shown to them, will not injure Allah in the least, but He will make their deeds of no effect
t.pickthall: Lo! those who disbelieve and turn from the way of Allah and oppose the messenger after the guidance hath been manifested unto them, they hurt Allah not a jot, and He will make their actions fruitless
t.arberry: Those who disbelieve and bar from God's way and make a breach with the Messenger after the guidance has become clear to them, they will nothing hurt God, and He will make their works to fail
t.hilali-khan: Verily, those who disbelieve, and hinder (men) from the Path of Allah (i.e. Islam), and oppose the Messenger (SAW) (by standing against him and hurting him), after the guidance has been clearly shown to them, they will not hurt Allah in the least, but He will make their deeds fruitless
t.itani: Those who disbelieve, and hinder from the path of God, and oppose the Messenger after guidance has become clear to them—they will not hurt God in the least, but He will nullify their deeds.
t.mubarakpuri: Verily, those who disbelieve, and hinder from the path of Allah, and oppose the Messenger, after the guidance has been clearly shown to them, they will not harm Allah in the least, but He will make their deeds fruitless,
t.qarib: The unbelievers who turn others away from the path of God and oppose the Messenger after guidance has become clear to them, will not hurt God in the least, but He will render their deeds worthless
t.wahiduddin: Surely, they who are bent on denying the truth and on barring [others] from the path of God, and oppose the Messenger when they have been shown guidance, cannot harm God in any way. He will cause all their deeds to come to nothing
t.shakir: Surely those who disbelieve and turn away from Allah's way and oppose the Apostle after that guidance has become clear to them cannot harm Allah in any way, and He will make null their deeds
t.haleem: Those who disbelieve, bar others from God’s path, and oppose the Messenger when they have been shown guidance, do not harm God in any way. He will make their deeds go to waste
t.maariful: Those who disbelieve and prevent (others) from Allah’s way, and oppose the Messenger, after the Guidance has appeared to them clearly, shall never bring harm to Allah in the least, and He will render their deeds vain
t.khattab: Indeed, those who disbelieve, hinder ˹others˺ from the Way of Allah, and defy the Messenger after ˹true˺ guidance has become clear to them; they will not harm Allah in the least, but He will render their deeds void
t.ghali: Surely the ones who have disbelieved and barred from the way of Allah and opposed the Messenger even after the guidance has become evident to them, they will never harm Allah anything, and He will soon frustrate their deeds
t.irving: The ones who disbelieve and obstruct God´s way, and oppose the Messenger even after guidance has been explained to them, will never injure God in any way while He will let their actions founder
t.iwq: Indeed, those who disbelieved and averted people from the path of Allah and opposed the Messenger after guidance had become clear to them - never will they harm Allah at all, and He will render worthless their deeds

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya orang-orang kafir dan (yang) menghalangi manusia dari jalan Allah serta memusuhi Rasul setelah petunjuk itu jelas bagi mereka, mereka tidak dapat memberi mudharat kepada Allah sedikitpun. Dan Allah akan menghapuskan (pahala) amal-amal mereka.
t_id.sabiq: Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang kafir dan menghalang-halangi manusia dari jalan Allah serta memusuhi rasul dengan congkak dan keras hati setelah petunjuk itu jelas bagi mereka, mereka tidak akan dapat memberi mudarat kepada Allah sedikit pun. Dan Allah akan menghapus semua amal perbuatan mereka.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya orang-orang kafir dan menghalang-halangi jalan Allah) jalan yang hak (dan memusuhi rasul) atau menentangnya (setelah petunjuk itu jelas bagi mereka) yang dimaksud petunjuk adalah jalan Allah tadi (mereka tidak dapat memberi mudarat kepada Allah sedikit pun. Dan Allah akan menghapuskan amal mereka) seperti sedekah dan lain-lainnya, yaitu pahalanya. Di akhirat kelak mereka tidak akan dapat melihat pahalanya. Ayat ini diturunkan berkenaan dengan orang-orang kafir yang terlibat dalam perang Badar, atau orang-orang kafir Bani Quraizhah dan Bani Nadhir.
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya orang-orang kafir dan (yang) menghalangi manusia dari jalan Allah serta memusuhi Rasul setelah petunjuk itu jelas bagi mereka, mereka tidak dapat memberi mudharat kepada Allah sedikitpun. Dan Allah akan menghapuskan (pahala) amal-amal mereka.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إن الذين كفروا وصدوا عن سبيل الله» طريق الحق «وشاقوا الرسول» خالفوه «من بعد ما تبيَّن لهم الهدى» هو معنى سبيل الله «لن يضروا الله شيئا وسيحبط أعمالهم» يبطلها من صدقة ونحوها فلا يرون لها في الآخرة ثوابا، نزلت في المطعمين من أصحاب بدر أو في قريظة والنضير
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ وَصَدُّواْ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ وَشَآقُّواْ ٱلرَّسُولَ مِنۢ بَعۡدِ مَا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُمُ ٱلۡهُدَىٰ لَن يَضُرُّواْ ٱللَّهَ شَيۡـٔٗا وَسَيُحۡبِطُ أَعۡمَٰلَهُمۡ (وَشَاقُّوا: خَالَفُوهُ، وَحَارَبُوهُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya orang-orang kafir dan menghalang-halangi jalan Allah) jalan yang hak (dan memusuhi rasul) atau menentangnya (setelah petunjuk itu jelas bagi mereka) yang dimaksud petunjuk adalah jalan Allah tadi (mereka tidak dapat memberi mudarat kepada Allah sedikit pun. Dan Allah akan menghapuskan amal mereka) seperti sedekah dan lain-lainnya, yaitu pahalanya. Di akhirat kelak mereka tidak akan dapat melihat pahalanya. Ayat ini diturunkan berkenaan dengan orang-orang kafir yang terlibat dalam perang Badar, atau orang-orang kafir Bani Quraizhah dan Bani Nadhir

ar: ۞ یَـٰۤأَیُّهَا ٱلَّذِینَ ءَامَنُوۤا۟ أَطِیعُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِیعُوا۟ ٱلرَّسُولَ وَلَا تُبۡطِلُوۤا۟ أَعۡمَـٰلَكُمۡ
ar_simple: ۞ يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَطِيعُوا اللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُوا الرَّسُولَ وَلَا تُبْطِلُوا أَعْمَالَكُمْ
transliteration: yāayyuhā alladhīna āmanū aṭīʿū l-laha wa-aṭīʿū l-rasūla walā tub'ṭilū aʿmālakum

t.sahih-int: O you who have believed, obey Allah and obey the Messenger and do not invalidate your deeds
t.asad: O you who have attained to faith! Pay heed unto God, and pay heed unto the Apostle, and let not your [good] deeds come to nought
t.yusuf-ali: O ye who believe! Obey Allah, and obey the messenger, and make not vain your deeds
t.pickthall: O ye who believe! Obey Allah and obey the messenger, and render not your actions vain
t.arberry: O believers, obey God, and obey the Messenger, and do not make your own works vain
t.hilali-khan: O you who believe! Obey Allah, and obey the Messenger (Muhammad SAW) and render not vain your deeds
t.itani: O you who believe! obey God, and obey the Messenger, and do not let your deeds go to waste.
t.mubarakpuri: O you who believe! Obey Allah, and obey the Messenger and invalidate not your deeds.
t.qarib: Believers, obey God and obey the Messenger and do not let your deeds go to waste
t.wahiduddin: Believers, obey God and obey the Messenger: do not let your deeds go to waste
t.shakir: O you who believe! obey Allah and obey the Apostle, and do not make your deeds of no effect
t.haleem: believers, obey God and the Messenger: do not let your deeds go to waste
t.maariful: O you who believe, obey Allah and obey the Messenger, and do not nullify your deeds
t.khattab: O believers! Obey Allah and obey the Messenger, and do not let your deeds be in vain
t.ghali: O you who have believed, obey Allah, and obey the Messenger, and do not void your deeds
t.irving: You who believe, obey God and obey the Messenger and do not leave your actions useless
t.iwq: O you who believe, obey Allah and obey the Messenger and do not invalidate your deeds

t_id.kemenag: Hai orang-orang yang beriman, taatilah Allah dan taatilah Rasul dan janganlah kamu merusakkan (pahala) amal-amalmu.
t_id.sabiq: Hai orang-orang yang beriman, taatlah kepada Allah dalam setiap apa yang diperintahkan kepada kalian. Taatlah juga kepada Rasul dalam setiap apa yang diserukannya. Dan janganlah kalian menghilangkan pahala amal perbuatan kalian.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Hai orang-orang yang beriman! Taatlah kepada Allah dan taatlah kepada rasul dan janganlah kalian merusakkan amal-amal kalian) dengan melakukan perbuatan-perbuatan maksiat, umpamanya.
t_id.iwq: Hai orang-orang yang beriman, taatilah Allah dan taatilah Rasul dan janganlah kamu merusakkan (pahala) amal-amalmu.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «يا أيها الذين آمنوا أطيعوا الله وأطيعوا الرسول ولا تبطلوا أعمالكم» بالمعاصي مثلا
tafsir.siraj-ar: ۞يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓاْ أَطِيعُواْ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُواْ ٱلرَّسُولَ وَلَا تُبۡطِلُوٓاْ أَعۡمَٰلَكُمۡ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Hai orang-orang yang beriman! Taatlah kepada Allah dan taatlah kepada rasul dan janganlah kalian merusakkan amal-amal kalian) dengan melakukan perbuatan-perbuatan maksiat, umpamanya

ar: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِینَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَصَدُّوا۟ عَن سَبِیلِ ٱللَّهِ ثُمَّ مَاتُوا۟ وَهُمۡ كُفَّارࣱ فَلَن یَغۡفِرَ ٱللَّهُ لَهُمۡ
ar_simple: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَصَدُّوا عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ثُمَّ مَاتُوا وَهُمْ كُفَّارٌ فَلَنْ يَغْفِرَ اللَّهُ لَهُمْ
transliteration: inna alladhīna kafarū waṣaddū ʿan sabīli l-lahi thumma mātū wahum kuffārun falan yaghfira l-lahu lahum

t.sahih-int: Indeed, those who disbelieved and averted [people] from the path of Allah and then died while they were disbelievers - never will Allah forgive them
t.asad: Verily, as for those who are bent on denying the truth and on barring [others] from the path of God, and then die as deniers of the truth - indeed, God will not grant them forgiveness
t.yusuf-ali: Those who reject Allah, and hinder (men) from the Path of Allah, then die rejecting Allah,- Allah will not forgive them
t.pickthall: Lo! those who disbelieve and turn from the way of Allah and then die disbelievers, Allah surely will not pardon them
t.arberry: Those who disbelieve and bar from God's way and then die disbelieving, them God will not forgive
t.hilali-khan: Verily, those who disbelieve, and hinder (men) from the Path of Allah (i.e. Islam); then die while they are disbelievers, Allah will not forgive them
t.itani: Those who disbelieve, and hinder from God’s path, and then die as disbelievers—God will not forgive them.
t.mubarakpuri: Verily, those who disbelieved and obstructed others from the path of Allah and then died as disbelievers -- never will Allah not forgive them.
t.qarib: The unbelievers who turn others away from the path of God and then die while they are unbelievers, God will not forgive them
t.wahiduddin: surely those who reject the truth and bar others from the path of God, then die as deniers of the truth, will not be granted forgiveness by God
t.shakir: Surely those who disbelieve and turn away from Allah's way, then they die while they are unbelievers, Allah will by no means forgive them
t.haleem: God will not forgive those who disbelieve, bar others from God’s path, and die as disbelievers
t.maariful: Those who disbelieved and prevented (others) from Allah’s way, then died as disbelievers, Allah will never forgive them
t.khattab: Surely those who disbelieve, hinder ˹others˺ from the Way of Allah, and then die as disbelievers; Allah will never forgive them
t.ghali: Surely the ones who have disbelieved and barred from the way of Allah, thereafter die (while) they are steadfast disbelievers, then Allah will never forgive them
t.irving: God will never pardon those who disbelieve and obstruct God is way, then die while they are disbelievers
t.iwq: Indeed, those who disbelieved and averted people from the path of Allah and then died while they were disbelievers - never will Allah forgive them

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya orang-orang kafir dan (yang) menghalangi manusia dari jalan Allah kemudian mereka mati dalam keadaan kafir, maka sekali-kali Allah tidak akan memberi ampun kepada mereka.
t_id.sabiq: Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang kafir dan menghalang-halangi orang lain untuk memeluk Islam lalu mati dalam keadaan kafir, maka Allah tidak akan memberi ampunan kepada mereka.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang kafir dan menghalangi jalan Allah) tuntunan-Nya, yaitu jalan petunjuk (kemudian mereka mati dalam keadaan kafir, maka sekali-kali Allah tidak akan memberi ampun kepada mereka) ayat ini diturunkan berkenaan dengan orang-orang kafir yang dikubur di Al-Qulaib, seusai perang Badar.
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya orang-orang kafir dan (yang) menghalangi manusia dari jalan Allah kemudian mereka mati dalam keadaan kafir, maka sekali-kali Allah tidak akan memberi ampun kepada mereka.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إن الذين كفروا وصدوا عن سبيل الله» طريقه وهو الهدى «ثم ماتوا وهم كفار فلن يغفر الله لهم» نزلت في أصحاب القليب
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ وَصَدُّواْ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ثُمَّ مَاتُواْ وَهُمۡ كُفَّارٞ فَلَن يَغۡفِرَ ٱللَّهُ لَهُمۡ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang kafir dan menghalangi jalan Allah) tuntunan-Nya, yaitu jalan petunjuk (kemudian mereka mati dalam keadaan kafir, maka sekali-kali Allah tidak akan memberi ampun kepada mereka) ayat ini diturunkan berkenaan dengan orang-orang kafir yang dikubur di Al-Qulaib, seusai perang Badar

ar: فَلَا تَهِنُوا۟ وَتَدۡعُوۤا۟ إِلَى ٱلسَّلۡمِ وَأَنتُمُ ٱلۡأَعۡلَوۡنَ وَٱللَّهُ مَعَكُمۡ وَلَن یَتِرَكُمۡ أَعۡمَـٰلَكُمۡ
ar_simple: فَلَا تَهِنُوا وَتَدْعُوا إِلَى السَّلْمِ وَأَنْتُمُ الْأَعْلَوْنَ وَاللَّهُ مَعَكُمْ وَلَنْ يَتِرَكُمْ أَعْمَالَكُمْ
transliteration: falā tahinū watadʿū ilā l-salmi wa-antumu l-aʿlawna wal-lahu maʿakum walan yatirakum aʿmālakum

t.sahih-int: So do not weaken and call for peace while you are superior; and Allah is with you and will never deprive you of [the reward of] your deeds
t.asad: AND SO, [when you fight in a just cause,] do not lose heart and [never] beg for peace: for, seeing that God is with you, you are bound to rise high [in the end]; and never will He let your [good] deeds go to waste
t.yusuf-ali: Be not weary and faint-hearted, crying for peace, when ye should be uppermost: for Allah is with you, and will never put you in loss for your (good) deeds
t.pickthall: So do not falter and cry out for peace when ye (will be) the uppermost, and Allah is with you, and He will not grudge (the reward of) your actions
t.arberry: So do not faint and call for peace; you shall be the upper ones, and God is with you, and will not deprive you of your works
t.hilali-khan: So be not weak and ask not for peace (from the enemies of Islam), while you are having the upper hand. Allah is with you, and will never decrease the reward of your good deeds
t.itani: So do not waver and call for peace while you have the upper hand. God is with you, and He will not waste your efforts.
t.mubarakpuri: So do not lose heart and beg for peace while you are superior. Allah is with you and He will never deprive you of (the reward of) your deeds.
t.qarib: So do not waiver and call for peace while you have the upper hand. God is with you. He will not waste your deeds
t.wahiduddin: So do not lose heart or appeal for peace when you have gained the upper hand. God is with you and will never let your works go to waste
t.shakir: And be not slack so as to cry for peace and you have the upper hand, and Allah is with you, and He will not bring your deeds to naught
t.haleem: So [believers] do not lose heart and cry out for peace. It is you who have the upper hand: God is with you. He will not begrudge you the reward for your [good] deeds
t.maariful: So, do not lose heart, and do not appeal for peace; you will be the uppermost, and Allah is with you; and He will never deprive you of your deeds
t.khattab: So do not falter or cry for peace, for you will have the upper hand and Allah is with you. And He will never let your deeds go to waste
t.ghali: So do not feel feeble and call for (your) submission while (Literally: and) you are the most exalted ones; and Allah is with you, and He will never divest you of your deeds
t.irving: So do not waver, and appeal for peace while you hold the upper hand. God is with you and will never let you be cheated in your actions
t.iwq: So do not weaken and call for peace while you are superior; and Allah is with you and will never deprive you of the reward of your deeds

t_id.kemenag: Janganlah kamu lemah dan minta damai padahal kamulah yang di atas dan Allah pun bersamamu dan Dia sekali-kali tidak akan mengurangi pahala amal-amalmu.
t_id.sabiq: Janganlah kalian lemah dalam menghadapi musuh, dan jangan mengajak mereka berdamai karena alasan takut kepada mereka, padahal kalian lebih tinggi dan akan menang dengan kekuatan iman. Allah selalu membela kalian dan Dia sekali-kali tidak akan mengurangi pahala amal perbuatan kalian.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Janganlah kalian lemah) merasa lemah (dan minta damai) dapat dibaca As-Salmi atau As-Silmi, artinya damai bersama dengan orang-orang kafir bila kalian bertemu dengan mereka dalam perang (padahal kalianlah yang di atas) lafal Al-A'launa asalnya adalah Al-A'lawuuna, kemudian Wawu Lam Fi'ilnya dibuang sehingga jadilah Al-A'launa, artinya, yang menang dan yang mengalahkan (dan Allah pun beserta kalian) yakni bantuan dan pertolongan-Nya (dan Dia sekali-kali tidak akan mengurangi) Allah tidak akan mengurangi kalian (amal-amal kalian) pahala amal-amal kalian.
t_id.iwq: Janganlah kamu lemah dan minta damai padahal kamulah yang di atas dan Allah pun bersamamu dan Dia sekali-kali tidak akan mengurangi pahala amal-amalmu.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فلا تهنوا» تضعفوا «وتدعوا إلى السَِّلم» بفتح السين وكسرها، أي الصلح مع الكفار إذا لقيتموهم «وأنتم الأعلوْن» حذف منه واو لام الفعل الأغلبون القاهرون «والله معكم» بالعون والنصر «ولن يَتِركُم» ينقصكم «أعمالكم» أي ثوابها
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَلَا تَهِنُواْ وَتَدۡعُوٓاْ إِلَى ٱلسَّلۡمِ وَأَنتُمُ ٱلۡأَعۡلَوۡنَ وَٱللَّهُ مَعَكُمۡ وَلَن يَتِرَكُمۡ أَعۡمَٰلَكُمۡ (فَلَا تَهِنُوا: لَا تَضْعُفُوا، وَتَجْبُنُوا عَنْ مُقَاتَلَةِ الكُفَّارِ, السَّلْمِ: الصُّلْحِ، وَالمُسَالَمَةِ, يَتِرَكُمْ أَعْمَالَكُمْ: يَنْقُصَكُمْ ثَوَابَ أَعْمَالِكُمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Janganlah kalian lemah) merasa lemah (dan minta damai) dapat dibaca As-Salmi atau As-Silmi, artinya damai bersama dengan orang-orang kafir bila kalian bertemu dengan mereka dalam perang (padahal kalianlah yang di atas) lafal Al-A'launa asalnya adalah Al-A'lawuuna, kemudian Wawu Lam Fi'ilnya dibuang sehingga jadilah Al-A'launa, artinya, yang menang dan yang mengalahkan (dan Allah pun beserta kalian) yakni bantuan dan pertolongan-Nya (dan Dia sekali-kali tidak akan mengurangi) Allah tidak akan mengurangi kalian (amal-amal kalian) pahala amal-amal kalian

ar: إِنَّمَا ٱلۡحَیَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنۡیَا لَعِبࣱ وَلَهۡوࣱۚ وَإِن تُؤۡمِنُوا۟ وَتَتَّقُوا۟ یُؤۡتِكُمۡ أُجُورَكُمۡ وَلَا یَسۡءَلۡكُمۡ أَمۡوَ ٰلَكُمۡ
ar_simple: إِنَّمَا الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا لَعِبٌ وَلَهْوٌ ۚ وَإِنْ تُؤْمِنُوا وَتَتَّقُوا يُؤْتِكُمْ أُجُورَكُمْ وَلَا يَسْأَلْكُمْ أَمْوَالَكُمْ
transliteration: innamā l-ḥayatu l-dun'yā laʿibun walahwun wa-in tu'minū watattaqū yu'tikum ujūrakum walā yasalkum amwālakum

t.sahih-int: [This] worldly life is only amusement and diversion. And if you believe and fear Allah, He will give you your rewards and not ask you for your properties
t.asad: The life of this world is but a play and a passing delight: but if you believe [in God] and are conscious of Him, He will grant you your deserts. And withal, He does not demand of you [to sacrifice in His cause all of] your possessions
t.yusuf-ali: The life of this world is but play and amusement: and if ye believe and guard against Evil, He will grant you your recompense, and will not ask you (to give up) your possessions
t.pickthall: The life of the world is but a sport and a pastime. And if ye believe and ward off (evil). He will give you your wages, and will not ask of you your wordly wealth
t.arberry: The present life is naught but a sport and a diversion; and if you believe and are godfearing, He will give you your wages, and will not ask of you your goods
t.hilali-khan: The life of this world is but play and pastime, but if you believe (in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism), and fear Allah, and avoid evil, He will grant you your wages, and will not ask you your wealth
t.itani: The life of this word is nothing but play and pastime. But if you have faith and lead a righteous life, He will grant you your rewards, and He will not ask you for your possessions.
t.mubarakpuri: This worldly life is only amusement and diversion. And if you believe and have Taqwa of Allah, He will grant you your rewards, and will not ask from you (to sacrifice all of) your possessions.
t.qarib: This worldly life is only amusement and pastime. But if you believe and become mindful of God, He will give you your rewards and not ask you for your properties
t.wahiduddin: The life of this world is only a game, a pastime, but if you believe and are mindful of God, He will recompense you and will not ask you for your wealth
t.shakir: The life of this world is only idle sport and play, and if you believe and guard (against evil) He will give you your rewards, and will not ask of you your possessions
t.haleem: the life of this world is only a game, a pastime, but if you believe and are mindful of God, He will recompense you. He does not ask you to give up [all] your posses-sions
t.maariful: The worldly life is but play and pastime. If you believe and be God-fearing, He will give you your rewards, and will not ask you your riches
t.khattab: This worldly life is no more than play and amusement. But if you are faithful and mindful ˹of Allah˺, He will grant you your ˹full˺ reward, and will not ask you ˹to donate all˺ your wealth
t.ghali: Surely the present life (Literally: the lowly life, i.e., the life of this world) is only playing and diversion; and in case you believe and be pious, He will bring you your rewards and will not ask of you your riches
t.irving: Worldly life is only a game and an amusement, If you believe and do your duty, your wages will be given you while your wealth will not be requested of you
t.iwq: This worldly life is only amusement and diversion. And if you believe and fear Allah, He will give you your rewards and not ask you for your properties

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya kehidupan dunia hanyalah permainan dan senda gurau. Dan jika kamu beriman dan bertakwa, Allah akan memberikan pahala kepadamu dan Dia tidak akan meminta harta-hartamu.
t_id.sabiq: Sesungguhnya kehidupan dunia ini hanyalah kepalsuan dan tipuan belaka. Jika kalian beriman, meninggalkan kemaksiatan dan mengerjakan kebaikan, maka Allah akan memberi kalian pahala dan Dia tidak akan meminta harta kepada kalian. Jika Dia meminta harta kepada kalian lalu mendesak kalian supaya memberikannya, kalian menjadi kikir. Dia akan menampakkan kedengkian kalian karena kalian sangat cinta kepada harta itu.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya kehidupan dunia) maksudnya, menyibukkan diri dalam kehidupan dunia (hanyalah permainan dan senda gurau. Dan jika kalian beriman serta bertakwa) kepada Allah, yang demikian itu adalah termasuk perkara akhirat (Allah akan memberikan pahala kepada kalian dan Dia tidak akan meminta harta-harta kalian) semuanya, melainkan hanya zakat yang diwajibkan.
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya kehidupan dunia hanyalah permainan dan senda gurau. Dan jika kamu beriman dan bertakwa, Allah akan memberikan pahala kepadamu dan Dia tidak akan meminta harta-hartamu.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إنما الحياة الدنيا» أي الاشتغال فيها «لعب ولهوٌ وإن تؤمنوا وتتقوا» الله وذلك من أمور الآخرة «يؤتكم أجوركم ولا يسألكم أموالكم» جميعها بل الزكاة المفروضة فيها
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّمَا ٱلۡحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنۡيَا لَعِبٞ وَلَهۡوٞۚ وَإِن تُؤۡمِنُواْ وَتَتَّقُواْ يُؤۡتِكُمۡ أُجُورَكُمۡ وَلَا يَسۡـَٔلۡكُمۡ أَمۡوَٰلَكُمۡ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya kehidupan dunia) maksudnya, menyibukkan diri dalam kehidupan dunia (hanyalah permainan dan senda gurau. Dan jika kalian beriman serta bertakwa) kepada Allah, yang demikian itu adalah termasuk perkara akhirat (Allah akan memberikan pahala kepada kalian dan Dia tidak akan meminta harta-harta kalian) semuanya, melainkan hanya zakat yang diwajibkan

ar: إِن یَسۡءَلۡكُمُوهَا فَیُحۡفِكُمۡ تَبۡخَلُوا۟ وَیُخۡرِجۡ أَضۡغَـٰنَكُمۡ
ar_simple: إِنْ يَسْأَلْكُمُوهَا فَيُحْفِكُمْ تَبْخَلُوا وَيُخْرِجْ أَضْغَانَكُمْ
transliteration: in yasalkumūhā fayuḥ'fikum tabkhalū wayukh'rij aḍghānakum

t.sahih-int: If He should ask you for them and press you, you would withhold, and He would expose your unwillingness
t.asad: [for,] if He were to demand of you all of them, and urge you, you would niggardly cling [to them], and so He would [but] bring out your moral failings
t.yusuf-ali: If He were to ask you for all of them, and press you, ye would covetously withhold, and He would bring out all your ill-feeling
t.pickthall: If He should ask it of you and importune you, ye would hoard it, and He would bring to light your (secret) hates
t.arberry: If He asks you for them, and presses you, you are niggardly, and He brings to light your rancour
t.hilali-khan: If He were to ask you of it, and press you, you would covetously withhold, and He will bring out all your (secret) ill-wills
t.itani: Were He to ask you for it, and press you, you would become tightfisted, and He would expose your unwillingness.
t.mubarakpuri: If He would demand of you all of it and urge you, you would withhold. And He will expose your (secret) ill--wills.
t.qarib: Were He to ask you for them and put you under pressure, you would become tightfisted, and He would expose your unwillingness
t.wahiduddin: If He were to ask it [wealth] of you, and continued to press you, you would be niggardly, and this would show your ill-will
t.shakir: If He should ask you for it and urge you, you will be niggardly, and He will bring forth your malice
t.haleem: you would be grudging if He were to ask you and press you for them, and He would bring your ill-will to light
t.maariful: If He were to ask you of it, and press you (to spend all your wealth), you would show miserliness, and He would expose your disgusts
t.khattab: If He were to do so and pressure you, you would withhold and He would bring out your resentment
t.ghali: In case He asks you for them, and so importune you, you would be miserly, and He would bring out your malice
t.irving: If He should ask you for it, and even dun you, you would act miserably and your grudges would become apparent
t.iwq: If He should ask you for them and press you, you would withhold, and He would expose your unwillingness

t_id.kemenag: Jika Dia meminta harta kepadamu lalu mendesak kamu (supaya memberikan semuanya) niscaya kamu akan kikir dan Dia akan menampakkan kedengkianmu.
t_id.sabiq: Sesungguhnya kehidupan dunia ini hanyalah kepalsuan dan tipuan belaka. Jika kalian beriman, meninggalkan kemaksiatan dan mengerjakan kebaikan, maka Allah akan memberi kalian pahala dan Dia tidak akan meminta harta kepada kalian. Jika Dia meminta harta kepada kalian lalu mendesak kalian supaya memberikannya, kalian menjadi kikir. Dia akan menampakkan kedengkian kalian karena kalian sangat cinta kepada harta itu.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Jika Dia memintanya dari kalian lalu mendesak kalian) mendesak meminta zakat tersebut (niscaya kalian akan kikir dan keluarlah) karena kekikiran kalian (kedengkian kalian) terhadap agama Islam.
t_id.iwq: Jika Dia meminta harta kepadamu lalu mendesak kamu (supaya memberikan semuanya) niscaya kamu akan kikir dan Dia akan menampakkan kedengkianmu.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إن يسألكموها فحيفكم» يبالغ في طلبها «تبخلوا ويخرج» البخل «أضغانكم» لدين الإسلام
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِن يَسۡـَٔلۡكُمُوهَا فَيُحۡفِكُمۡ تَبۡخَلُواْ وَيُخۡرِجۡ أَضۡغَٰنَكُمۡ (فَيُحْفِكُمْ: يُلِحَّ عَلَيْكُمْ، وَيُجْهِدْكُمْ, أَضْغَانَكُمْ: أَحْقَادَكُمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Jika Dia memintanya dari kalian lalu mendesak kalian) mendesak meminta zakat tersebut (niscaya kalian akan kikir dan keluarlah) karena kekikiran kalian (kedengkian kalian) terhadap agama Islam

ar: هَـٰۤأَنتُمۡ هَـٰۤؤُلَاۤءِ تُدۡعَوۡنَ لِتُنفِقُوا۟ فِی سَبِیلِ ٱللَّهِ فَمِنكُم مَّن یَبۡخَلُۖ وَمَن یَبۡخَلۡ فَإِنَّمَا یَبۡخَلُ عَن نَّفۡسِهِۦۚ وَٱللَّهُ ٱلۡغَنِیُّ وَأَنتُمُ ٱلۡفُقَرَاۤءُۚ وَإِن تَتَوَلَّوۡا۟ یَسۡتَبۡدِلۡ قَوۡمًا غَیۡرَكُمۡ ثُمَّ لَا یَكُونُوۤا۟ أَمۡثَـٰلَكُم
ar_simple: هَا أَنْتُمْ هَٰؤُلَاءِ تُدْعَوْنَ لِتُنْفِقُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ فَمِنْكُمْ مَنْ يَبْخَلُ ۖ وَمَنْ يَبْخَلْ فَإِنَّمَا يَبْخَلُ عَنْ نَفْسِهِ ۚ وَاللَّهُ الْغَنِيُّ وَأَنْتُمُ الْفُقَرَاءُ ۚ وَإِنْ تَتَوَلَّوْا يَسْتَبْدِلْ قَوْمًا غَيْرَكُمْ ثُمَّ لَا يَكُونُوا أَمْثَالَكُمْ
transliteration: hāantum hāulāi tud'ʿawna litunfiqū fī sabīli l-lahi faminkum man yabkhalu waman yabkhal fa-innamā yabkhalu ʿan nafsihi wal-lahu l-ghaniyu wa-antumu l-fuqarāu wa-in tatawallaw yastabdil qawman ghayrakum thumma lā yakūnū amthālakum

t.sahih-int: Here you are - those invited to spend in the cause of Allah - but among you are those who withhold [out of greed]. And whoever withholds only withholds [benefit] from himself; and Allah is the Free of need, while you are the needy. And if you turn away, He will replace you with another people; then they will not be the likes of you
t.asad: Behold, [O believers,] it is you who are called upon to spend freely in God’s cause: but [even] among you are such as turn out to be niggardly! And yet, he who acts niggardly [in God’s cause] is but niggardly towards his own self: for God is indeed self-sufficient, whereas you stand in need [of Him]; and if you turn away [from Him], He will cause other people to take your place, and they will not be the likes of you
t.yusuf-ali: Behold, ye are those invited to spend (of your substance) in the Way of Allah: But among you are some that are niggardly. But any who are niggardly are so at the expense of their own souls. But Allah is free of all wants, and it is ye that are needy. If ye turn back (from the Path), He will substitute in your stead another people; then they would not be like you
t.pickthall: Lo! ye are those who are called to spend in the way of Allah, yet among you there are some who hoard. And as for him who hoardeth, he hoardeth only from his soul. And Allah is the Rich, and ye are the poor. And if ye turn away He will exchange you for some other folk, and they will not be the likes of you
t.arberry: Ha, there you are; you are called upon to expend in God's way, and some of you are niggardly. Whoso is niggardly is niggardly only to his own soul. God is the All-sufficient; you are the needy ones. If you turn away, He will substitute another people instead of you, then they will not be your likes
t.hilali-khan: Behold! You are those who are called to spend in the Cause of Allah, yet among you are some who are niggardly. And whoever is niggardly, it is only at the expense of his ownself. But Allah is Rich (Free of all wants), and you (mankind) are poor. And if you turn away (from Islam and the obedience of Allah), He will exchange you for some other people, and they will not be your likes
t.itani: Here you are, being called to spend in the cause of God. Among you are those who withhold; but whoever withholds is withholding against his own soul. God is the Rich, while you are the needy. And if you turn away, He will replace you with another people, and they will not be like you.
t.mubarakpuri: Here you are now invited to spend in Allah's cause; but among you are those who withhold (stingily). And whoever acts stingily is but stingy toward himself. For Allah is indeed free of needs, while you are the needy. And if you turn away, He will replace you with other people; then they will not be like you.
t.qarib: Here you are, called upon to spend in the cause of God. Among you are those who are tightfisted; but whoever withholds only withholds from his own soul. God is the Rich Beyond Need, while you are the needy. And if you turn away, He will replace you with another people, and they will not be the likes of you
t.wahiduddin: Behold! You are those who are called upon to spend for God's cause, but among you are those who are niggardly, and whoever stints does so against his own self. Indeed, God is self-sufficient, but you stand in need [of Him], and if you turn back, He will bring in your place another people, who will not be like you
t.shakir: Behold! you are those who are called upon to spend in Allah's way, but among you are those who are niggardly, and whoever is niggardly is niggardly against his own soul; and Allah is Self-sufficient and you have need (of Him), and if you turn back He will bring in your place another people, then they will not be like you
t.haleem: though now you are called upon to give [a little] for the sake of God, some of you are grudging. Whoever is grudging is so only towards himself: God is the source of wealth and you are the needy ones. He will substitute other people for you if you turn away, and they will not be like you
t.maariful: Yes, you are such that you are called upon to spend in Allah’s way, but some of you withhold in miserliness- and he who withholds in miserliness withholds against himself- and Allah is the Need-Free, and you are the needy. And if you turn away, He will replace you by some other people, then they will not be like you
t.khattab: Here you are, being invited to donate ˹a little˺ in the cause of Allah. Still some of you withhold. And whoever does so, it is only to their own loss. For Allah is the Self-Sufficient, whereas you stand in need ˹of Him˺. If you ˹still˺ turn away, He will replace you with another people. And they will not be like you
t.ghali: Now, you are these called to expend in the way of Allah. Then among you (some) are miserly; and whoever is miserly, then he is miserly only upon himself. And Allah is the Ever-Affluent, (Literally: Ever-Rich) and you are the poor ones. And in case you turn away, He will (readily) exchange you for another people; thereafter they will not be your likes
t.irving: Here you are, those who are called upon to spend in God´s way, even though some of you are miserly! Anyone who acts niggardly is miserly only so far as his own soul is concerned. God is Transcendent while you are poor. If you should turn away [from the call of duty and belief], He will replace you with some other folk who then will not be like you at all
t.iwq: Here you are - those invited to spend in the cause of Allah - but among you are those who withhold out of greed. And whoever withholds only withholds benefit from himself; and Allah is the Free of need, while you are the needy. And if you turn away, He will replace you with another people; then they will not be the likes of you

t_id.kemenag: Ingatlah, kamu ini orang-orang yang diajak untuk menafkahkan (hartamu) pada jalan Allah. Maka di antara kamu ada yang kikir, dan siapa yang kikir sesungguhnya dia hanyalah kikir terhadap dirinya sendiri. Dan Allah-lah yang Maha Kaya sedangkan kamulah orang-orang yang berkehendak (kepada-Nya); dan jika kamu berpaling niscaya Dia akan mengganti (kamu) dengan kaum yang lain; dan mereka tidak akan seperti kamu ini.
t_id.sabiq: Ingatlah, kalian ini adalah orang-orang yang diajak untuk menginfakkan harta di jalan Allah. Lalu di antara kalian ada yang kikir dan tidak mau menginfakkan hartanya. Barangsiapa yang kikir berarti ia telah merugikan dirinya sendiri. Allah Mahakaya sedang kalian adalah orang-orang miskin yang membutuhkan- Nya. Dan jika kalian tidak taat kepada Allah, niscaya Dia akan mengganti kalian dengan kaum lain yang tidak seperti kalian yang tidak menaati-Nya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Ingatlah kalian) wahai, kalian ingatlah (kalian ini orang-orang yang diajak untuk menafkahkan harta kalian pada jalan Allah) maksudnya untuk menafkahkan apa yang telah diwajibkan atas kalian, yaitu zakat. (Maka di antara kalian ada orang yang kikir, dan siapa yang kikir sesungguhnya dia hanyalah kikir terhadap dirinya sendiri) lafal Bakhila dapat bermuta'addikan 'Ala atau 'An, untuk itu dapat dikatakan Rakhila 'Alaihi dan Bakhila 'Anhu. (Dan Allahlah Yang Maha Kaya) artinya, tidak membutuhkan infak kalian (sedangkan kalianlah orang-orang yang berhajat) kepada-Nya (dan jika kalian berpaling) dari taat kepada-Nya (niscaya Dia akan mengganti kalian dengan kaum yang lain) Dia akan menjadikan yang lain sebagai pengganti kalian (dan mereka tidak akan seperti kalian) tidak akan berpaling dari taat kepada-Nya, bahkan mereka benar-benar akan taat kepada-Nya.
t_id.iwq: Ingatlah, kamu ini orang-orang yang diajak untuk menafkahkan (hartamu) pada jalan Allah. Maka di antara kamu ada yang kikir, dan siapa yang kikir sesungguhnya dia hanyalah kikir terhadap dirinya sendiri. Dan Allah-lah yang Maha Kaya sedangkan kamulah orang-orang yang berkehendak (kepada-Nya); dan jika kamu berpaling niscaya Dia akan mengganti (kamu) dengan kaum yang lain; dan mereka tidak akan seperti kamu ini.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ها أنتم» يا «هؤلاء تدعون لتنفقوا في سبيل الله» ما فرض عليكم «فمنكم من يبخل ومن يبخلْ فإنما يبخل عن نفسه» يقال بخل عليه وعنه «والله الغني» عن نفقتكم «وأنتم الفقراء» إليه «وإن تتولوا» عن طاعته «يستبدل قوما غيركم» أي يجعلهم بدلكم «ثم لا يكونوا أمثالكم» في التولي عن طاعته بل مطيعين له عز وجل
tafsir.siraj-ar: هَـٰٓأَنتُمۡ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ تُدۡعَوۡنَ لِتُنفِقُواْ فِي سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ فَمِنكُم مَّن يَبۡخَلُۖ وَمَن يَبۡخَلۡ فَإِنَّمَا يَبۡخَلُ عَن نَّفۡسِهِۦۚ وَٱللَّهُ ٱلۡغَنِيُّ وَأَنتُمُ ٱلۡفُقَرَآءُۚ وَإِن تَتَوَلَّوۡاْ يَسۡتَبۡدِلۡ قَوۡمًا غَيۡرَكُمۡ ثُمَّ لَا يَكُونُوٓاْ أَمۡثَٰلَكُم
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Ingatlah kalian) wahai, kalian ingatlah (kalian ini orang-orang yang diajak untuk menafkahkan harta kalian pada jalan Allah) maksudnya untuk menafkahkan apa yang telah diwajibkan atas kalian, yaitu zakat. (Maka di antara kalian ada orang yang kikir, dan siapa yang kikir sesungguhnya dia hanyalah kikir terhadap dirinya sendiri) lafal Bakhila dapat bermuta'addikan 'Ala atau 'An, untuk itu dapat dikatakan Rakhila 'Alaihi dan Bakhila 'Anhu. (Dan Allahlah Yang Maha Kaya) artinya, tidak membutuhkan infak kalian (sedangkan kalianlah orang-orang yang berhajat) kepada-Nya (dan jika kalian berpaling) dari taat kepada-Nya (niscaya Dia akan mengganti kalian dengan kaum yang lain) Dia akan menjadikan yang lain sebagai pengganti kalian (dan mereka tidak akan seperti kalian) tidak akan berpaling dari taat kepada-Nya, bahkan mereka benar-benar akan taat kepada-Nya
